name: Arimoclomol NPC open-label extension supported longer-term disease and safety context
summary: The NPC-002 open-label extension adds longer-term disease-progression and safety context for arimoclomol, but remains NPC-specific and open-label.
statement: In the NPC-002 open-label extension, arimoclomol-treated Niemann-Pick disease type C participants had longer-term clinical severity follow-up consistent with sustained slowing of disease progression, with no new safety concerns reported.
population_or_model: Participants with Niemann-Pick disease type C who completed the double-blind NPC-002 trial and entered the open-label extension
time_horizon: Open-label follow-up through 48 months after the double-blind phase
quantitative_note: Of 50 participants who started the double-blind phase, 41 entered the open-label extension and 29 completed 48 months; mean 5DNPCCSS and R4DNPCCSS scores increased by 3.2 and 2.7 over 48 months, respectively, and switchers from placebo showed lower annual score changes after starting arimoclomol.
caveats: The extension was open-label after the randomized phase and does not provide an independent randomized durability trial.
The evidence is concentrated in NPC and should not be generalized to broad aging or LEV-relevant proteostasis restoration.
Several authors were affiliated with the product sponsor.
No new safety concerns in the extension does not resolve chronic preventive-use safety in aging populations.
